name: Brown Rice

book_claims.claim: Induces greater satiety than white rice, making overeating difficult
book_claims.mechanism: Fibre-rich cell wall slows gastric emptying and small bowel transit, triggering early satiety signals

book_claims.claim: Contains 3x more fibre than white rice (3g vs 1g per cup)
book_claims.mechanism: Fibre-rich cell wall restricts rate of starch digestion and absorption; slow glucose release reduces insulin spikes

book_claims.claim: Part of a sample daily diet providing 73g fibre in only 1886 kcal with complete nutrition
book_claims.mechanism: 2 cups brown rice + 1 cup lentils + 20 almonds + vegetables + fruit + olive oil + flax seeds = 73g fibre, 76g protein, 64g fat

supplementary_research.finding: A 2010 study in Archives of Internal Medicine (PMID 20548009) found that replacing 50g/day of white rice with brown rice was associated with a 16% lower risk of type 2 diabetes. Replacing the same amount with whole grains reduced risk by 36%.

A 2014 RCT (Br J Nutr, PMID 24158434) found that substituting brown rice for white rice significantly reduced postprandial blood glucose and insulin in overweight Asian Indians, consistent with the book's glycemic control claims.

A 2019 systematic review (Nutrients, PMID 31619630) found that brown rice consumption was associated with improved glycemic control, lipid profiles, and body weight management compared to white rice across multiple trials.
A 2016 BMJ meta-analysis (PMID 27301975) found whole grain intake associated with dose-dependent reductions in CVD mortality, cancer mortality, and all-cause mortality.

culinary_pairings.source: Classic combination for complete protein (mujaddara, dal-chawal)
Nutty fat complements grain flavor; improves nutrient absorption
Foundation of bibimbap, fried rice, grain bowls
Traditional genmai with miso soup for balanced meal
Anti-inflammatory spice in pilaf and biryani preparations
Classic rice and beans for complete protein

seasonality: Available year-round as a dried pantry staple. Short-grain, long-grain, and basmati varieties each suit different preparations.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Three times the fiber of white rice. Mix of soluble and insoluble fiber supports gut microbiome diversity.
Cofactor for superoxide dismutase and other antioxidant enzymes. Partially reduced by phytic acid but still significant.
Important for insulin sensitivity and over 300 enzymatic reactions. Lost in white rice milling.
Unique to rice bran. Reduces cholesterol absorption and has antioxidant activity. Completely absent in white rice.
Concentrated in bran and germ layers removed during white rice milling. Brown rice retains 80% more B vitamins.

synergies.description: Brown rice + lentils provides complete protein with all essential amino acids
Complementary amino acid profiles; grains supply methionine, legumes supply lysine
